title: lycopodioides cochleata
intro: Lycopodioides cochleata, also known as club moss, is a type of ancient vascular plant that belongs to the Lycopodiaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Lycopodioides cochleata include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating urinary tract infections and kidney stones. Lycopodioides cochleata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
